findings.description: Tables A1 and A2 appear to be duplicated in the HTML. The table data first appears in a compact, bordered rendering (HTML pages 2-4) and then appears a second time in a spacious, differently-styled rendering (HTML pages 5-7). A reader scrolling through the HTML top-to-bottom would encounter the full Table A1 data (K1-K62, ENVRTO, ENVRCO) twice and Table A2 data twice, disrupting reading flow.
findings.evidence: HTML page 4 ends with Table A2 emission rates for K1-K10 in compact format. HTML page 5 then restarts with 'Table A1. Modeled Release Parameters for Point Sources' heading and K1 data in a spacious format, re-presenting all Table A1 data that was already shown in HTML pages 2-4. Similarly, Table A2 emission rate data is shown again in HTML pages 6-7 after already appearing in chunk 4.

findings.description: Tables A1 and A2 are rendered both as embedded page images AND as separate HTML elements, creating real content duplication. Five
